candidate.label: hip_tile_segment_dblock_headsplit_accumulator
candidate.contract: prepacked tile-local compressed-group segments, one CTA per segment and D block, split H64 across lanes, reduce in shared memory, emit one compressed dKV D16 atomic row per segment
candidate.build_change: file-based PyTorch extension load for this minimal path so the HIP translation unit does not include torch/extension.h; single gfx950 target is forced by environment

read: This proves the coalesced-write model alone is insufficient. The route/group-owned D-block reducer gets the desired 23.8x modeled global-write reduction, but it moves the producer to a segment-major shape that reloads/recomputes q/dO contribution work instead of preserving the fast query-major producer. This mirrors the route-owned TileLang whole-boundary diagnosis: final dKV writes are not the dominant problem in that schedule; the producer shape is.

decision.next_contract: token/head/query-major CTA like the Triton fast path, sweep selected groups while q and dO are hot, then perform group-aware lane/warp/block segmented reduction in the epilogue before reduced global dKV atomics or compact partial rows
decision.reason: Only a query-major producer can keep the 1.9-2.8 ms recompute/contribution body; the group-aware reduction must be inside that producer's epilogue, not the outer grid owner.
